refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure timely processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide real-time monitoring and alerting of latency breaches to Safety Services.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use low-latency network protocols and optimized data encoding for teleoperation communication.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes within the NGHE solution to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams in the network link.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms in Safety Services.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use low-latency network protocols and optimized data encoding for teleoperation communication.

potential_sources: Interaction between network protocol optimization and vehicle communication hardware.
Dependency on Safety Services for latency alerting.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 is logically decoupled from other DPs by focusing on communication protocol optimization; dependencies are manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with existing vehicle communication hardware.
Risk that latency improvements may not fully meet p95 ≤ 150 ms under peak load.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 (edge computing) if DP1 alone does not meet latency targets.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing teleoperation communication protocols, command processing, and telemetry handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation commands and telemetry data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send telemetry data to Safety Services and NGHE Solution monitoring components.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands from operator control modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but less focused on teleoperation protocol specifics.
Vehicle hardware handles command execution but not communication protocol optimization.
Primary module for teleoperation communication logic and latency management.
Monitors latency alerts but does not implement communication protocols.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation communication logic in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves latency management.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
Risk of module overload if additional responsibilities are added without clear boundaries.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with NGHE Solution and Safety Services to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (low-latency protocols) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing teleoperation latency with clear verification metrics.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency monitoring responsibilities overlap with Safety Services.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has high containment score and low diffusion, appropriate for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end via telemetry and network link monitoring within assigned IM boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication protocol implementation to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface definitions between Teleoperation and Safety Services for latency alerting.
Monitor potential overload of Teleoperation module responsibilities in future decompositions.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the FR refinement can be further detailed to explicitly separate network and processing latency components, and DP selection should consider fallback or complementary DPs (e.g., edge computing).
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize network transmission latency for teleoperation commands.
FR2: Minimize processing latency within teleoperation communication modules.
FR3: Provide latency breach detection and alerting to Safety Services.

revise.why: To improve clarity and completeness of latency sources and solution approaches before further IM assignments.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can be enhanced to handle latency monitoring.
open_risks: Latency targets may not be met under peak network load.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under representative network conditions.
Validation of protocol compatibility with vehicle hardware.
